Gestion pratique de la violence et de l'agressivité du patient et de son entourage
Pour les professionnels de santé hospitaliers et en cabinet, une approche humaine qui préserve la relation patient-soignant.

Cette formation d'All Leaders Initiative est dédiée aux professionnels de santé confrontés à la **violence en milieu hospitalier ou en ville**. Le contexte est alarmant : la violence dans les hôpitaux a progressé de 10 % entre 2023 et 2024, 37 % des soignants hospitaliers déclarent subir régulièrement des agressions physiques, et ce chiffre monte à 84 % chez les aides-soignants. Les soignants sont les primo-répondants dans 59 % des cas de violence — avant même les services de sécurité.

### Une expertise reconnue
Face à cette réalité, All Leaders Initiative a développé une expertise spécifique aux environnements de soins à risque augmenté — urgences, psychiatrie, gériatrie, soins à domicile, SMUR — reconnue et accréditée par le Ministère de la Santé italien dans le cadre de la formation continue des personnels soignants (ECM).

### Approuvée par les professionnels
La formation de 16 heures (2 jours) forme les équipes à gérer le stress du patient et de son entourage, à communiquer de façon tactique pour désamorcer, à se protéger physiquement sans jamais porter de coup, et à intervenir en équipe selon un protocole commun. Elle préserve en toutes circonstances la relation soignant-patient comme socle éthique inaltérable. L'évaluation inclut un retour à froid à 3 mois.

Bien-être psychique et santé mentale au travail
Programme dédié à la santé mentale comme fondement de la QVCT et de la performance globale.

**All Leaders Initiative** propose un ensemble de modules articulables en parcours pour répondre à l'obligation légale des entreprises en matière de santé mentale *(Code du Travail, art. 4121)* et aller au-delà, jusqu'à en faire un axe stratégique de performance durable. Le constat est préoccupant : la part des arrêts maladie liés à la santé mentale est passée de 11 % en 2016 à 20 % en 2022, et 81 % des salariés considèrent le sujet comme un enjeu majeur.

### Cinq dimensions complémentaires 
1. **Ingénierie de dispositifs internes** : conception et mise en œuvre de solutions spécifiques pour soutenir le bien-être psychique.
2. **Sensibilisation de toutes les populations** : actions visant à informer et former tous les niveaux de l'organization.
3. **Formations au maintien du bien-être psychique** : sessions destinées à développer les compétences en matière de santé mentale.
4. **Premiers secours en santé mentale (PSSM France)** : accompagnement pour identifier et répondre aux signes de détresse.
5. **Animation de communautés de référents internes** : création d'espaces de partage et d'appui mutuel au sein des équipes.

### Différents formats
Les modules proposés vont de la session de sensibilisation de 2 heures à la formation de 2 jours sur le bien-être mental et le travail, en passant par un atelier stratégique pour aider les dirigeants et RH à définir la posture de leur organization sur le sujet. Un dispositif de renforcement continu — demi-journées mensuelles puis trimestrielles — permet de faire vivre une communauté interne de référents dans la durée.

Programme de 100 jours pour impulser une nouvelle dynamique managériale, garantissant subsidiarité, responsabilisation et autonomie.

Le **coaching OKR collectif** est un programme exclusif co-développé par All Leaders Initiative et STEPPPS, conçu pour impulser une nouvelle dynamique managériale en 100 jours. Il constitue le chaînon manquant entre le coaching individuel isolé, les temps forts d'équipe ponctuels et les grands projets de transformation globale.

### Combinaison de deux approches 
Les **OKR** (Objectives & Key Results), qui structurent des objectifs précis et mesurables sur un cycle court, et le **Leadership basé sur l'intention (LBI)**, qui pousse la prise de décision au plus près de l'action. Chaque participant formule trois niveaux d'intention — descendante (comment incarner l'objectif commun), montante (ses engagements et besoins) et personnelle (ce qui rend la démarche soutenable pour lui). Cette mécanique organise une véritable rencontre des intentions à tous les niveaux de l'organization.

### 5 temps forts accompagnés par des coachs sur 3 mois avec des plans d'action individualisés
Il favorise subsidiarité, responsabilisation et autonomie sans sacrifier le contrôle opérationnel. Les bénéfices organizationnels sont concrets : alignement stratégique top-down et bottom-up, cycles d'amélioration rapides, et ancrage d'une culture de confiance et de transparence.

A shared, global, systemic and objective assessment of the key dimensions of the structures and dynamics of a team or organization and its collective effectiveness.

All Leaders Initiative offers a **collective effectiveness assessment** designed to objectively establish the strengths and barriers of a team or organization in a global and systemic way. It addresses various situations: identification of a leadership team's development areas, preparation for a merger or reorganization, overcoming collective blockages, or taking up post as a new leader.

### Two complementary approaches
1. The **qualitative approach** is based on semi-structured individual interviews analysing the structural components of activity — leadership, vision, culture, relational processes — drawing on Berne organizational Theory (B.O.T.), a branch of Transactional Analysis dedicated to organizations.
2. The **quantitative approach** uses an online survey of all employees to measure the gap between current experience and aspirations, between actual management culture and intended strategic intention. This component benefits from a privileged partnership with OpinionWay, of which an All Leaders Initiative associate is consulting director for the Workplace Transformations division.

The two approaches can be combined and supplemented by a financial analysis of hidden costs, to anchor the assessment in a ROI logic.

A mechanism to identify, quantify, understand and analyse the causes and effects of workplace conflicts in order to define levers for their transformation into sources of positive energy and development for organizations and their employees, with massive gains in costs and internal efficiency.

**All Leaders Initiative** co-developed with **OpinionWay** from 2021 the first national observatory on the cost of workplace conflict. The figures are striking: an equivalent of one month's work per year is lost to conflict, over 10 billion euros of salary cost are dissipated each month, and 48% of employees observe signs of aggression or violence in their conflict situations.

### Operational diagnostic tool
This approach transforms this observatory into an operational diagnostic tool for organizations. It functions as a mirror: data from the national reference framework — 1,000 interviews representative of public and private sectors — are compared with results from an online survey conducted internally among employees. Four themes are explored: experience of conflict, causes and effects, management methods, and the link with psychological safety and performance.

### Conflict costs quantified and financially objectified
The assessment produces a financial evaluation of the cumulative cost of conflicts, based on time actually lost, and creates an objective and shared vision across all stakeholders — HR, Finance, managers, employee representatives. It leads to identification of hidden gain opportunities directly exploitable, often more accessible than external growth levers, and opens to dedicated support modules.
A mechanism to identify, quantify, understand and analyse the causes and effects of workplace conflicts in order to define levers for their transformation.
Conflict costs quantified and financially objectified
Proven international approach to manage conflict in the short term and protect yourself in case of direct confrontation.

The **conflict management** training from All Leaders Initiative is designed for any professional exposed to aggression, regardless of sector, physical condition or level of safety experience. It starts from a neurological finding: under stress, the brain no longer reasons, it reacts. Theorising without training is pointless. The pedagogy employed is inspired by emergency medicine — train quickly, train usefully, train so it holds under pressure.

### Comprehensive programme
The 16-hour programme (2 days) covers three complementary axes: technical tools for verbal and physical conflict, collective intervention protocol, and individual stress management via the TOP method. The physical component relies on using the aggressor's clothing — no strikes delivered — with intensity adjustable depending on the aggressor profile. The tools are immediately applicable and accessible to all, without any sporting prerequisites.

### Discomfort and hyper-realism
The pedagogy combines discomfort stimulation, hyper-realistic scenarios, individual and team work, then hot and cold evaluation at 3 months. The objective is not to train self-defence experts but to anchor protective reflexes adapted to the professional context, with total respect for the aggressor and the legal framework.

All Leaders Initiative offers **corporate seminars and teambuildings** of 1 to 2 days built entirely bespoke, based on a prior diagnosis of the team's explicit and implicit issues. The approach does not start from a catalogue of services but from an alliance with the leader: listening, co-construction of the programme, then facilitation by certified professional coaches on behalf of the client.

### Three dimensions
Each seminar articulates three complementary dimensions:
1. operations (work on projects, shared vision),
2. regulation (work on relationships and collective processes),

The balance between these three dimensions is an integral part of the strategy built with the sponsor.

### Support over time
Support does not end at the seminar's conclusion. All Leaders Initiative systematically plans follow-up points at 1 and 3 months to sustain the effects over time, and can design successive major moments if the transformation challenge justifies it. The ultimate goal is to help the leader develop their own capacity to grow their team's collective intelligence, beyond the one-off intervention.

All Leaders Initiative propose un **diagnostic de l'efficacité collective** conçu pour objectiver de manière globale et systémique les forces et les freins d'une équipe ou d'une organization. Il s'adresse à des situations variées : identification des zones de développement d'une équipe de direction, préparation d'une fusion ou réorganization, dépassement de blocages collectifs, ou prise de poste d'une nouvelle direction.

Experiential Leadership in Virtual Reality – Mission One
Simulator to break silos, develop psychological safety and create a culture of accountability.

**Mission One** is a 2-day immersive virtual reality (VR) training offered by All Leaders Initiative to develop leadership, cohesion and decision-making under pressure. The scenario places two crews — one aboard a submarine, the other in a combat helicopter — facing a common mission to accomplish with limited resources, time constraints and restricted communication channels. Cooperation is not optional: it is the only path to success.

### Iterative approach
Each VR simulation sequence is followed by in-depth debriefings led by experienced leaders, allowing analysis of behaviours, decryption of team dynamics and progressive construction of more effective collective strategies. The equipment is entirely mobile — it can be deployed in the client's premises or at a seminar venue — and the standard configuration is 2 teams of 7 people with dedicated support (mentor, mediator, game master).

### Target audience
Mission One is designed for executive committees, operational managers and cross-functional project teams whose performance depends on rapid decision-making and flawless communication in situations of uncertainty. A streamlined one-day format, the flash assessment, is available for initial evaluation.

**All Leaders Initiative** propose la **Virtual Box**, un stage résidentiel de 2,5 jours conçu pour préparer les professionnels à faire face à l'agressivité en milieu de travail. Dans un contexte où la violence professionnelle est en hausse — touchant jusqu'à 8 % des travailleurs européens selon les secteurs —, cette formation répond à une réalité quotidienne pour les équipes en contact avec le public, le secteur médical, les transports, la sécurité ou le management de terrain.

### Approche reposant sur l'immersion
Il est impossible de savoir comment on réagit face à l'agressivité sans l'avoir expérimentée. Le programme alterne mises en situation progressives, entraînements pratiques et débriefings analytiques. Les participants explorent les mécanismes de l'escalade émotionnelle, de la déshumanisation et du basculement vers la violence — du point de vue de l'agressé comme de l'agresseur.  

### Formation encadrée par deux experts complémentaires
une psychothérapeute-coach spécialisée dans le travail émotionnel et la résilience, et un expert en self-défense et gestion des conflits sous stress. Ce double accompagnement garantit un cadre physiquement et psychologiquement sécurisé, permettant un apprentissage sans risque de traumatisme.  


Les bénéfices pour l'organization sont concrets : réduction des arrêts de travail, amélioration du bien-être, limitation du turnover et des risques juridiques.

Gestion des conflits physiques et verbaux
Approche éprouvée à l'international pour gérer les conflits dans le temps court et se protéger en cas de confrontation directe.

La formation **Gestion des conflits** d'All Leaders Initiative s'adresse à tout professionnel exposé à l'agressivité, quel que soit son secteur, sa condition physique ou son niveau d'expérience en sécurité. Elle part d'un constat neurologique : sous stress, le cerveau ne raisonne plus, il réagit. Théoriser sans entraîner ne sert à rien. La pédagogie mise en place s'inspire de la médecine d'urgence — former vite, former utile, former pour que ça tienne sous pression.

### Un programme complet
Le programme de 16 heures (2 jours) travaille trois axes complémentaires : les outils techniques du conflit verbal et physique, le protocole d'intervention collectif, et la gestion du stress individuel via la méthode TOP. La partie physique repose sur l'utilisation des vêtements de l'agresseur — aucun coup porté — avec une intensité modulable selon le profil de l'agresseur. Les outils sont immédiatement applicables et accessibles à tous, sans condition de sportivité.

### Inconfort et ultra-réalisme
La pédagogie combine stimulation par l'inconfort, mises en situation ultraréalistes, travail seul et en équipe, puis évaluation à chaud et à froid à 3 mois. L'objectif n'est pas de former des experts en self-défense mais d'ancrer des réflexes protecteurs adaptés au contexte professionnel, dans le respect total de l'agresseur et du cadre légal.

Méthode issue du monde militaire pour gérer son stress et rester performant. Formation qualifiante pour l'accès au niveau Praticien.

Les **Techniques d'Optimisation du Potentiel (TOP)** sont un ensemble de méthodes éducatives — non thérapeutiques — développées à l'origine par l'armée française sous la direction du Dr Edith Perreaut-Pierre. All Leaders Initiative les intègre à son ingénierie pédagogique et les propose en formation initiale qualifiante, ouvrant l'accès au parcours Praticien des écoles TOP françaises.

### 3 méta objectifs
La méthode repose sur trois méta-objectifs complémentaires : 
1. **se dynamiser** avant l'action (mobiliser l'énergie physique et mentale), 
2. **se réguler** pendant ou après (revenir au calme, réduire la tension musculaire), 
3. **récupérer efficacement** (restaurer un niveau d'activation compatible avec l'activité). 

Elle s'appuie sur le travail de la respiration, de la relaxation et de la visualisation — des outils que chaque participant sélectionne et adapte pour construire sa propre boîte à outils personnelle.

### Polyvalence extrême
Extrêmement polyvalentes, les TOP s'appliquent aussi bien à la gestion du stress au quotidien qu'aux situations de haute pression, de conflit ou de fatigue nerveuse intense. Elles constituent le socle personnel et la dimension individuelle de plusieurs autres formations proposées par All Leaders Initiative, notamment dans les programmes de gestion des conflits.

Les formations sont dispensées par Jérôme Bouteiller, Praticien TOP, et Marie Emmanuelle Py, Formation Initiale TOP, tous deux formés par Optimiser Mon Potentiel.

Leadership expérientiel en réalité virtuelle – Mission One
Simulateur pour briser les silos, développer la sécurité psychologique et créer une culture de la responsabilité.

**Mission One** est une formation immersive en réalité virtuelle (VR) de 2 jours proposée par All Leaders Initiative pour développer le leadership, la cohésion et la prise de décision sous pression. Le scénario place deux équipages — l'un à bord d'un sous-marin, l'autre dans un hélicoptère — face à une mission commune à accomplir avec des ressources limitées, des contraintes de temps et des canaux de communication restreints. La coopération n'est pas une option : c'est la seule voie vers le succès.

### Dispositif itératif 
Chaque séquence en simulation VR est suivie de débriefings approfondis animés par des leaders expérimentés, qui permettent d'analyser les comportements, décrypter les dynamiques d'équipe et construire progressivement des stratégies collectives plus efficaces. L'équipement est entièrement mobile — il se déploie dans les locaux du client ou en lieu de séminaire — et la configuration nominale est de 2 équipes de 7 personnes avec un encadrement dédié (mentor, médiateur, maître du jeu).

### Public visé
Mission One s'adresse aux comités de direction, aux managers opérationnels et aux équipes projet transverses dont la performance repose sur la décision rapide et la communication sans faille en situation d'incertitude. Coaching dédié aux femmes leaders, pour s'autoriser, se légitimer et développer son leadership dans des environnements historiquement masculins.

All Leaders Initiative propose un programme dédié au **leadership féminin**, fondé sur une conviction centrale : en théorie, le leadership ne devrait pas être genré ; en pratique, il l'est encore. L'enjeu n'est pas de revendiquer une différence, mais de réhabiliter la diversité des postures — celles qui conjuguent force et douceur, ambition et sens, affirmation sans domination.

### Une offre singulière
La singularité de l'offre repose sur la transmission par l'expérience vécue. Les programmes sont animés par des femmes au parcours pionnier : officières de marine, expertes en sûreté nucléaire, dirigeantes issues d'environnements ultra-techniques et à haute responsabilité. Ce sont des praticiens authentiques du pouvoir dans des univers historiquement masculins, pas des théoriciens du sujet.

### Coaching intégratif
L'approche s'inspire du coaching intégratif de **Vincent Lenhardt**, de la philosophie humaniste d'**André Comte-Sponville** et de la pensée de **Simon Sinek**, articulée autour de trois piliers : le sens, l'action et l'ancrage — aligner tête, coeur et corps. Les formats sont variés : coachings individuels pour dirigeantes, parcours collectifs favorisant la sororité professionnelle, séminaires mixtes sur la coopération homme-femme, et conférences inspirantes.

### Un bénéfice qui dépasse le seul public féminin
Développer le leadership féminin, c'est faire évoluer les modèles de pouvoir et de réussite de toute l'organization.

Collective space to transform managerial challenges into success strategies with the power of the group.

The **managerial supervision groups** offered by All Leaders Initiative enable colleagues at equivalent levels to meet 2 to 4 times per year in a confidential setting facilitated by an experienced professional coach, to share their challenges, pool their experiences and build success strategies together. The approach is directly inspired by supervisions practised by coaching professionals and masterclasses for entrepreneurs.

### Circles of trust
Each session welcomes a limited number of participants gathered in a circle of trust: each person can present a professional case, benefit from the collective perspective of the group, and learn from situations brought by others. The external coach ensures structure, confidentiality and impartiality — their sector knowledge allows them to bring fresh perspectives without being caught up in the organization's internal dynamics.

### Retention lever
Beyond individual benefits — faster problem-solving, skills development, stronger trust and leadership — the approach acts as a retention lever: few organizations offer such a peer development space. The tools used (CODEV, De Bono's hats, structured brainstorming) are also modelling tools: each participant can reuse them with their own team.

Mental Wellbeing and Mental Health at Work
Programme dedicated to mental health as the foundation for work quality of life and overall performance.

**All Leaders Initiative** offers a set of modular modules that can be articulated into a pathway to meet the legal obligation of organizations regarding mental health *(Labour Code, article 4121)* and go beyond, making it a strategic axis for sustainable performance. The findings are concerning: the proportion of sick leave related to mental health rose from 11% in 2016 to 20% in 2022, and 81% of employees consider the subject a major issue.

### Five complementary dimensions
1. **Internal solution engineering**: design and implementation of specific solutions to support mental wellbeing.
2. **Awareness-raising for all populations**: activities to inform and train all levels of the organization.
3. **Mental wellbeing maintenance training**: sessions designed to develop mental health skills.
4. **Mental Health First Aid (PSSM France)**: support to identify and respond to signs of distress.
5. **Internal referent community management**: creation of spaces for sharing and mutual support within teams.

### Different formats
The modules range from a 2-hour awareness session to a 2-day mental wellbeing and work training, through to a strategic workshop to help leaders and HR define their organization's position on the subject. A continuous reinforcement mechanism — half-day monthly then quarterly sessions — enables an internal referent community to be sustained over time.

Mesure du coût de la conflictualité

Un dispositif pour identifier, quantifier, comprendre et analyser les causes et les effets des conflits au travail afin de définir les leviers de leur transformation en sources d'énergie positive et de développement pour les organizations et leurs collaborateurs, avec des gains massifs de coûts et d'efficience internes.

**All Leaders Initiative** a co-développé avec **OpinionWay** dès 2021 le premier observatoire national du coût des conflits au travail. Les chiffres sont saisissants : l'équivalent d'un mois de travail par an est perdu en conflits, plus de 10 milliards d'euros de coût salarial sont dissipés chaque mois, et 48 % des salariés observent des manifestations d'agressivité ou de violence dans leurs situations conflictuelles.

### Outil de diagnostic opérationnel
Ce dispositif transforme cet observatoire en outil de diagnostic opérationnel pour les organizations. Il fonctionne en miroir : les données du référentiel national — 1 000 interviews représentatives du secteur public et privé — sont confrontées aux résultats d'une enquête en ligne menée en interne auprès des collaborateurs. Quatre thématiques sont explorées : l'expérience des conflits, leurs causes et effets, les modalités de gestion, et le lien avec la sécurité psychologique et la performance.

### Coût des conflits chiffré et financièrement objectivé
Le diagnostic produit une évaluation financière du coût cumulé des conflits, basée sur le temps effectivement perdu, et crée une vision objective et partagée entre toutes les parties prenantes — DRH, DAF, managers, représentants du personnel. Il débouche sur l'identification de gisements de gains cachés directement exploitables, souvent plus accessibles que les leviers externes de croissance, et ouvre sur des modules d'accompagnement dédiés.

Le **diagnostic flash Mission One** est la version concentrée et accessible de l'expérience [VR Mission One](/accompagnements/leadership-development/leadership-experientiel-en-realite-virtuelle-mission-one) : une journée d'immersion en réalité virtuelle pour obtenir une évaluation rapide, objective et précise de la dynamique interne d'une équipe, avant tout engagement dans un programme de développement plus long.

### Un excellent révélateur
En plaçant les participants dans les conditions d'un **sous-marin** et d'un **hélicoptère de combat** en coopération, le dispositif révèle en situation réelle trois dimensions clés : l'efficacité de la communication inter-équipes (flux, surcharges, blocages), le niveau d'entente et de cohésion (zones de friction, qualité des relations), et les patterns de leadership en action (comment les leaders animent la décision et l'initiative).

### Un diagnostic complet en une journée
En une journée, l'équipe repart avec une lecture claire de ses points forts et de ses axes de travail prioritaires — un point de départ objectif et partagé pour orienter les interventions de développement à venir. Le format est utilisé aussi bien comme outil de diagnostic pur que comme déclencheur opérationnel d'une dynamique collective nouvelle.

OKR Collective Coaching – STEPPPS & Intent-Based Leadership
100-day programme to drive new managerial momentum, guaranteeing subsidiarity, accountability and autonomy.

**Collective OKR coaching** is an exclusive programme co-developed by All Leaders Initiative and STEPPPS, designed to drive new managerial momentum in 100 days. It represents the missing link between isolated individual coaching, occasional team high-impact moments and large-scale global transformation projects.

### Combination of two approaches
**OKR** (Objectives & Key Results), which structure precise and measurable objectives over a short cycle, and **Intent-Based Leadership (IBL)**, which pushes decision-making closer to action. Each participant formulates three levels of intention — downward (how to embody the common objective), upward (their commitments and needs) and personal (what makes the approach sustainable for them). This mechanism organises a genuine meeting of intentions at all levels of the organization.

### 5 major milestones supported by coaches over 3 months with personalised action plans
It promotes subsidiarity, accountability and autonomy without sacrificing operational control. The organizational benefits are concrete: top-down and bottom-up strategic alignment, rapid improvement cycles, and embedding a culture of trust and transparency.

Ateliers sur mesure pour transformer l'inertie en initiative et le conformisme en leadership authentique, avec intelligence émotionnelle et sécurité psychologique.

All Leaders Initiative propose un **parcours de développement pour une montée en compétences managériales**, conçus sur mesure pour des groupes de 6 à 18 managers, en intra ou interentreprises. L'ambition dépasse la transmission de techniques : il s'agit de travailler la posture intérieure du manager — son style de leadership authentique, son intelligence émotionnelle, sa capacité relationnelle — autant que ses outils pratiques.

### Six piliers complémentaires
1. le **leadership de l'hôte** (affirmer son propre style plutôt qu'imiter), 
2. la **communication interpersonnelle** (signes de reconnaissance, échanges constructifs), 
3. les **fondamentaux de la performance d'équipe** (inclusion, sécurité psychologique), 
4. l'**intelligence émotionnelle et situationnelle**, 
5. une **boîte à outils managériale personnalisée** (délégation, feedback, gestion du temps, réunions), 
6. la **sécurité psychologique** comme socle de l'engagement durable.

### Pédagogie
La pédagogie alterne apports théoriques, exercices en binôme et en sous-groupes, visualisations, réflexions individuelles et techniques d'optimisation du potentiel (TOP). Les bénéfices organizationnels sont documentés : 
- réduction du turnover, 
- amélioration de la cohésion, 
- alignement stratégique renforcé et diminution des silos. 

Les impacts individuels portent sur l'assertivité, la résilience, la maîtrise relationnelle et la prévention de l'épuisement.

Practical Management of Patient Violence and Aggression and Their Surroundings
For hospital and practice healthcare professionals, a human approach that preserves the patient-carer relationship.

This All Leaders Initiative training is dedicated to healthcare professionals facing **violence in hospital or community settings**. The context is alarming: violence in hospitals increased by 10% between 2023 and 2024, 37% of hospital healthcare workers report regularly experiencing physical assault, and this figure rises to 84% among care assistants. Healthcare workers are first responders in 59% of violence cases — even before security services.

### Recognised expertise
Faced with this reality, All Leaders Initiative has developed specific expertise in high-risk care environments — emergency departments, psychiatry, geriatric care, home care, SMUR — recognised and accredited by the Italian Ministry of Health as part of ongoing training for healthcare personnel (ECM).

### Endorsed by professionals
The 16-hour training (2 days) trains teams to manage patient and family stress, communicate tactically to defuse situations, protect themselves physically without ever striking, and intervene as a team according to a common protocol. It preserves the carer-patient relationship as an unalterable ethical foundation in all circumstances. Evaluation includes a follow-up review at 3 months.

Mastering Your Aggression and Others' — Professional Aggression Management Virtual Box
Complete immersion in an environment of physical and psychological discomfort to master your aggression and that of others.

**All Leaders Initiative** offers the **Virtual Box**, a 2.5-day residential training designed to prepare professionals to face workplace aggression. In a context where professional violence is on the rise — affecting up to 8% of European workers across sectors — this training responds to a daily reality for teams in contact with the public, healthcare, transport, security or field management.

### Immersion-based approach
It is impossible to know how you will react to aggression without having experienced it. The programme alternates progressive scenarios, practical training and analytical debriefings. Participants explore the mechanisms of emotional escalation, dehumanisation and the shift towards violence — from both the perspectives of the aggressed and the aggressor.

### Training led by two complementary experts
A psychotherapist-coach specialised in emotional work and resilience, and an expert in self-defence and conflict management under stress. This dual support ensures a physically and psychologically safe framework, enabling learning without risk of trauma.


The benefits for the organization are concrete: reduction in sick leave, improved wellbeing, reduced turnover and legal risks.

PSSM Training – Mental Health First Aid
Fundamental knowledge of the most common psychological disorders and an action plan to adopt appropriate behaviour.

All Leaders Initiative offers **PSSM France training** (Premiers Secours en Santé Mentale — Mental Health First Aid), a standardised and educational programme based on 20 years of international experience, initiated in Australia and deployed in France by officially accredited trainers. The objective: to train internal referents capable of identifying signals of psychological distress, adopting appropriate behaviour and referring to competent professionals — by direct analogy with physical first aid.

### Interactive format and eligible for funding
In two days of in-person training, participants acquire fundamental knowledge of the most common disorders — depressive, anxiety, psychotic, substance-related — as well as a concrete action plan to intervene in crisis or non-crisis situations. The pedagogy is active: realistic scenarios, videos, testimonies, and provision of an individual manual. The training is eligible for funding as professional training.

### Dual value-add
The value-add of All Leaders Initiative is twofold: PSSM accredited trainers who are also experienced consultants, coaches and mediators in business, and a systematic follow-up review (3 to 6 months later) enabling measurement of the actual implementation of learning and delivery of a report to the sponsor.

### In-house pricing
€3,500 excl. VAT for 8 participants, follow-up review included (€250 excl. VAT per additional participant, up to 16). Cross-company sessions available in Paris.

Séminaire co-construit après diagnostic précis, pour agir sur les enjeux réels de l'équipe.

All Leaders Initiative propose des **séminaires d'entreprise et teambuildings** de 1 à 2 jours construits intégralement sur mesure, à partir d'un diagnostic préalable des enjeux explicites et implicites de l'équipe. La démarche ne part pas d'un catalogue de prestations mais d'une alliance avec le responsable : écoute, co-construction du programme, puis animation déléguée par le client à des coachs professionnels certifiés.

### Trois dimensions
Chaque séminaire articule trois dimensions complémentaires : 
1. les opérations (travail sur les projets, la vision partagée), 
2. la régulation (travail sur les relations et les processus collectifs) 
3. la formation (apports ciblés en management ou développement des compétences). 

La répartition entre ces trois dimensions fait partie intégrante de la stratégie construite avec le commanditaire.

### Accompagnement dans le temps
L'accompagnement ne s'arrête pas à la fin du séminaire. All Leaders Initiative prévoit systématiquement des points de suivi à 1 et 3 mois pour cultiver les effets dans la durée, et peut concevoir plusieurs temps forts successifs si l'enjeu de transformation le justifie. L'objectif final est d'aider le responsable à développer sa propre capacité à faire grandir l'intelligence collective de son équipe, au-delà de l'intervention ponctuelle.

Développe l'autonomie du leader sur la durée
Method from the military world to manage stress and remain performant. Qualifying training for access to Practitioner level.

**Techniques d'Optimisation du Potentiel (TOP)** are a set of educational — non-therapeutic — methods originally developed by the French military under the direction of Dr Edith Perreaut-Pierre. All Leaders Initiative integrates them into its educational engineering and offers them as qualifying initial training, opening access to the Practitioner pathway of French TOP schools.

### 3 meta-objectives
The method is based on three complementary meta-objectives:
1. **energise** before action (mobilise physical and mental energy),
2. **regulate** during or after (return to calm, reduce muscle tension),
3. **recover effectively** (restore an activation level compatible with the activity).

It relies on breathing work, relaxation and visualisation — tools that each participant selects and adapts to build their own personal toolkit.

### Extreme versatility
Extremely versatile, TOP techniques apply equally to everyday stress management and high-pressure situations, conflict or intense nervous fatigue. They form the personal foundation and individual dimension of several other trainings offered by All Leaders Initiative, particularly in conflict management programmes.

Training is delivered by Jérôme Bouteiller, TOP Practitioner, and Marie Emmanuelle Py, TOP Initial Training, both trained by Optimiser Mon Potentiel.

Coaching dedicated to women leaders, to give yourself permission, legitimise yourself and develop your leadership in historically male-dominated environments.

All Leaders Initiative offers a **women's leadership programme** founded on a central conviction: in theory, leadership should not be gendered; in practice, it still is. The challenge is not to claim a difference, but to rehabilitate the diversity of postures — those that combine strength and gentleness, ambition and meaning, affirmation without domination.

### Singular offering
The offering's singularity rests on transmission through lived experience. Programmes are led by women with pioneering careers: naval officers, nuclear safety experts, leaders from ultra-technical and highly responsible environments. These are authentic practitioners of power in historically male-dominated worlds, not theorists of the subject.

### Integrative coaching
The approach is inspired by the integrative coaching of **Vincent Lenhardt**, the humanist philosophy of **André Comte-Sponville** and the thinking of **Simon Sinek**, articulated around three pillars: meaning, action and grounding — aligning head, heart and body. Formats are varied: individual coaching for female leaders, collective pathways promoting professional sisterhood, mixed seminars on male-female cooperation, and inspiring conferences.

### A benefit that extends beyond the female audience alone
Developing women's leadership is about evolving the power and success models of the entire organization.

Acting as a strategist in a volatile and uncertain world
Intent-based leadership and subsidiarity to navigate volatility. Lessons from Admiral Vandier on flexible strategic decision-making.

## How can we avoid being caught up in the contagion of chaos and enable our organizations to "act
as strategists in a volatile and uncertain world?" Insights from Admiral Vandier, General Officer of
the Armed Forces, whose strategic and pragmatic thinking we particularly appreciate, and whose
embodiment of Intent-Based Leadership is exemplary. ### By embracing the paradox of "the urgency of
the long term" By making strategic decisions quickly... but flexibly! This seems like not a double
but a triple constraint... In reality, determining long-term intentions as early as possible, while
ensuring the ability to adapt to rapid change, guarantees the best capacity for choice. And this can
only be done as close to the ground as possible, by multi-disciplinary teams. Welcome to
subsidiarity! ### By embracing complexity There is no point trying to reduce it – it's a losing
battle and increases the sense of loss of control. Instead, take advantage of new digital tools to
"tame" as much data as possible, in all fields, by multiplying perspectives – this can be seen as a
windfall. As long as you keep the prism of the major objectives you are pursuing in order to decide
without being overwhelmed by the abundance of information. ### By giving up the illusory ambition of
controlling risks How? Paradoxically: by deciding to take them... That is, by daring to _"make
choices that are not strictly planned or framed"_ by the organization's actors, based on a general
intention, shared and understood by all. Intention serving subsidiarity. > **_"This means allowing
the organization's actors to decide based on a 'general intention' given by management but without
necessarily strict directives or precise orders"_** > > Admiral Pierre Vandier These lessons drawn
from chaos management illustrate the essential nature of **Intent-Based Leadership** and
subsidiarity, which are dear to **All Leaders Initiative**. In form, they also show that in the face
of chaos, threats and uncertainty, it is possible not to suffer but to learn, anticipate, and even
take advantage of the potential of the situation. **Chaos is navigable, and some even find pleasure
in it!** * * * #### Admiral Vandier's article published by HBR France [available online
here](https://www.hbrfrance.fr/strategie/face-a-la-contagion-du-chaos-definir-une-nouvelle-grille-daction-60384)
or [downloadable in full PDF
format](/documents/2312_Amiral-Vandier-une-nouvelle-grille-daction-HBR-Fr.pdf).
APPRECIATIVE INQUIRY: DISCOVERING THE STRENGTHS, VALUES AND WINNING BEHAVIOURS OF AN organization
Appreciative approach to amplify an organization's strengths. Discover the 7 principles of Appreciative Inquiry to transform your teams.
APPRECIATIVE INQUIRY: DISCOVERING THE STRENGTHS, VALUES...

Appreciative Inquiry is an organizational development approach. It focuses on the strengths and resources of an organization rather than its problems and shortcomings. It aims to create virtuous change by identifying and amplifying the positive aspects of the organization. Appreciative Inquiry is based on the conviction that organizations have a natural capacity to improve. Particularly when they focus on what is working well and actively involve organization members in the change process. It emphasises discovery, co-creation and collective engagement. It provides a framework for facilitating dialogue, reflection and positive action. This is not semantic sleight of hand but a genuine constructivist process that builds on an organization's strengths and the resources of each of its members to affirm its healthy, energetic and evolving side. It has proven itself in varied settings, including high-risk environments, and completes or even takes a central place in interventions aimed at increasing an organization's performance and resilience. ## THE 7 PRINCIPLES OF APPRECIATIVE INQUIRY 1. ### **1 - The constructivist principle:**
"Our words construct the world". 2. ### **2 - The simultaneity principle:**
As soon as we question the current facts, we provoke change. 3. ### **3 - The poetic principle:**
organizations offer many subjects for study. The one we choose gives a representation of the organization. 4. ### **4 - The anticipatory principle:**
The more positive the images, the more positive the action. 5. ### **5 - The positive principle:**
Positive questions generate positive change. 6. ### **6 - The modelling principle:**
To achieve real change, you must embody it. The process of change is itself alive. 7. ### **7 - The free choice principle:**
People are more effective when they are free to choose what to contribute and how to contribute.
Stop waiting for solutions, foster intention!
Replace the command 'bring solutions' with 'share your intention'. Foster transparency and co-creation in your teams.

## Because "coming with solutions" can be counterproductive, we at All Leaders Initiative prefer to emphasise the concept of intention. ### Why? Because coming with solutions may be ill-suited to our complex environments. Here are **3 arguments** that challenge the relevance of the classic injunction "don't come with problems, but with solutions" #### The most obvious: it does not encourage transparency. Except in simple or slightly complicated cases (in which case dialogue with the boss is more like "reporting with veto rights"?), the tendency would rather be to keep the problem to yourself, until it takes up too much space and becomes visible. Yet what we increasingly seek is to obtain as many weak signals as possible so we can anticipate as early as possible. #### Secondly, coming with solutions assumes you have first properly identified the problem. The temptation is already great to reduce the complexity of facts by simplifying. With a tendency to put a solution opposite the problem, and stick to it (confirmation bias). In this case, it seems more virtuous to come with a problem and first focus on discussing its contours. #### Coming with one or more solutions implies that there are ideal solutions (like solving an equation). It is more prudent to maintain doubt about this and discuss, collectively, the proposals and their impact on the problem. And that will be the moment to start an exchange of intentions, the manager explaining his general intention (the framework, his "desired final effect" to use military terminology) and his collaborator explaining theirs (his "idea of manoeuvre"). Moreover, intention implies real ownership of the action by its sender.

Instead of "don't come with a problem but with solutions", we encourage substituting:

"Come with your problem and your intention".

Concretely, it is about encouraging reflection before action in a virtuous spiral like this: - What are the facts? - What do I think about it, what seems desirable to me? - What are the options and constraints? - Finally, what is my intention? Is it truly aligned with the overall intention (vision and objectives) of my team/organization? #### At All Leaders Initiative, we call this "the Leadership Scale (based on intention)" but we can discuss it further!
Kindness, authenticity and other 'catch-all words' to avoid. Really?
Kindness is not an empty word: translated into concrete behaviours, it becomes essential to the protective framework of a high-performing team.
Kindness, authenticity and other 'catch-all words' to...

## "Kindness", "authenticity", "suspension of judgement", "listening", "respect"… ### We often hear that these are hackneyed, worn-out words that no longer mean anything… And yet... When we support a team and work together to define **a framework that is both protective and demanding**, which will allow them to **go further together**, these words are almost always present. And when we ask the person who proposed one of them what it means for them, they always know how to answer precisely what they put behind it, and why it's important to them. This is often complemented with the vision of a few others who appropriate it in a different way, but no one raises their eyes to heaven... #### The problem is not the words, it's how they're appropriated. When they remain slogans, of course they seem empty. But as soon as you translate them into concrete behaviours, on which you can align and commit yourself, they become **essential**. In management, they are the key to what Jim Collins calls _"a protective framework"_ and Simon Sinek _"a circle of security"_. **Such a framework is not "nice", it's essential.** Because it protects the team from unspoken issues, power plays and simmering tensions. And without it, there is no real and lasting performance. #### It's not so much the words that matter as what we do with them. If these words manage to withstand criticism, it's because they are foundational in that they address real and living needs. So how can we restore substance to the word? - By specifying how the person using it defines it - By linking it to desired behaviour - By having the whole group validate it - By putting it at the service of a concrete objective
To those who complain about always hearing the same words, we say, not as a challenge but as an opening for working together: > _"You who deplore that kindness (at random…) no longer means anything, what concretely do you put behind the word?_ > _And what do you observe in your environment that is not aligned with this?_ > _And above all, how would you like this to be more present, really within your team and organization?"_

**And you, do you still dare to use these kinds of words in business?** #### We create the conditions for teams to work powerfully together, that's our job. We avoid overusing trendy words (sometimes a bit of Anglicisms…), don't give up on the great classics of theoretical references (it's practical…), and especially mobilise what comes from the teams themselves!
What coaching practice has unlearned me!
Discover the professional coaching posture: stop giving ready-made answers and guide your coachees towards their own solutions.

## What coaching practice has unlearned me... or what is professional coaching really? ### Our
co-founder partner Marie Emmanuelle Py tells all! #### Professional coaching is a helping
relationship with a very specific form: It is about: > _**Helping a person, team or organization,
through one or more interventions, to find or build their own solutions, with a view to development
that is both sustainable and global.**_ Most of us think we can do this when asked for help on a
subject. For my part, I have learned to help in different ways. Not simple to transcribe simply, but
here is an attempt: _(I am deliberately not discussing here the difference between a therapeutic
relationship and a coaching relationship, which I will develop specifically)_ As a **friend**, I
help by listening and trying to understand, by supporting, by being with, by encouraging, by
offering another perspective on the situation or even by giving some advice. However, it is
difficult for me to remain neutral and not get involved: this is the famous sympathy. As a
**mother**, I help through my unconditional love, by transmitting a system of values that seems to
me to be the best, and I accompany "as best as I can" the different challenges of children's lives,
aiming for their balance and autonomy, someday. It is also difficult for me not to be caught up in
intense emotions, or to accept from them views that go against my value system. As a **manager** or
**project leader**, I help by establishing clear goals in an organization as transparent as possible
and by enabling everyone to develop as an object of production but also as a person, in their
individual and collective dynamics. I am however responsible for the result and it is often
difficult for me not to have a preference for the colleagues who are progressing best and not to be
caught up in daily emergencies. As an **expert** or **trainer**, I help... by answering the
questions I am asked and by sharing my experience according to precise pedagogical engineering.
During the allotted time, it is often difficult for me to take the time to confront my solutions
with the requester's point of view: I am rather in the explanation mode. As a **mentor**, I place
enormous importance on the character and daily lives of my "mentees". I aim to help them grow in
confidence and leadership. However, it is difficult for me not to have a "project" or ambition for
them since their success is in a way my success. I imagine it's the same for **consultants**. ###
There are ingredients in all these helping relationships that make a good professional coach. It is
really in the very particular assembly of these that the "formula" lies which allows our coachees to
remain fully responsible for their choices and their development solutions. Indeed in what precedes,
we can see two main trends: - That of knowing better for others what is good for them (high
position) - That of wanting to help the other excessively (rescuer position) ### The coaching
posture Indeed, what we learn during our coaching training is above all a **posture**, which allows
us to have a type of relationship as profound as possible (alliance), objectives as clear as
possible (those of the coachee, without induction on our part), and at the same time no control over
them. Tools and techniques are important and differentiating but they are…only tools. It all depends
on how they are used. ### Here is what I had to (un)learn to do: - **Stop giving ready-made answers
or direct solutions**: I know that people have the answers to their own challenges. My role as a
coach is to guide them towards their own understanding and their own solutions, by asking powerful
questions that lead them to think deeply. - **Free myself from a certain directive tendency,
result-oriented**: people are more likely to be motivated and engaged when they are actors in their
own decisions and when they dare to take initiative. I aim as much or more for the empowerment
process as for the content. - **Change perspective.** I learned to seek the perspective of my
coachee(s), to truly understand their map of the world. Stop trying to solve problems but to enable
aspirations and find their own solutions. I try to replace "problems" as much as possible with
"challenges", "issues" or "aspirations". And this is not cosmetic: nothing like basing myself on a
person or team's strengths or past successes to help them visualise their dreams and develop a
concrete action plan to achieve them. - **Let go of the action plan**. The coaching process is
co-creative. I trust each client's unique path and respect their development pace. Again, I am
responsible for the process, for my neutrality (supported by therapy and supervision practice) and
for the quality of our relationship. My coachee is responsible for their choices. Professional
coaching practice has allowed me to question the way I help the people who come to see me. Of
course, I continue to be a friend, mother, expert, … which are great ways to support someone. But to
be able to claim to do professional coaching, I had to learn a posture, to set a secure framework,
and above all unlearn wanting to help a person more, or differently, than what they are ready to do
by desire or degree of maturity. I had to unlearn doing the work, answering, pleasing or pleasing
myself by finding suitable solutions. I had to learn to trust the other person, no matter their
degree of suffering or frustration, as much as I trust myself. And I have only had beautiful
encounters on this path. **Discover our support** in [individual
coaching](/accompagnements/coaching-de-managers/coaching-individuel-pour-managers/) or in
[collective coaching](/accompagnements/coaching-de-managers/ateliers-collectifs-coaching-equipes/).

In crisis, organizations must react AND build the future. Lessons from an explorer on adaptation and leadership in uncertainty.

## organizations must be "simultaneously reacting to past crises and building the future". Related
to this, the very interesting interview by Nastasia Hadjadji with **Christian Clot**, explorer and
researcher, on the need to adapt in the event of crisis rather than aiming for a return to the
initial state, and to anticipate the major paradigm shifts to come. We do not agree with his
criticism of the notion of resilience, although we do agree that it is not sufficient for it to
"serve as a compass" for organizations. Restoring resilience to its status as a process of returning
to a healthy situation (and not necessarily a previous one) rather than seeing it as a competence
(which you have or don't) does relativise this criticism. His 3 major recommendations from
**Christian Clot** for the company correspond to an interesting vision of **safety culture** [(learn
more about safety
culture)](/ressources/idees/le-role-majeur-de-la-culture-de-surete-pour-anticiper-des-risques): 1. 1
– "shift managers into leaders" 2. 2 – train these leaders to take and accept risk by leveraging the
latest neuroscience research 3. 3 – rely on a vigilance unit "entirely dedicated to the study of
weak signals" We particularly like the lessons he draws from his years of exploration in extreme
situations, including the one that calls for **"modelling your capacity for wonder"** to rely on it
in times of difficulty. This is not unlike the notion of **"psychological capital"** developed in
particular by American researcher in behavioural science and organizational specialist **Fred
Luthans** and which significantly explains both performance and well-being at work… (see an overview
of the notion of [Psychological
capital](https://positivepsychology.com/psychological-capital-psycap/))
